good audit experience. first job in kpmg. provides good training experience for audit. however, depending on the team and engagement, it can be a bit overwhelming. there will be times you need to work overtime.

good place to learn. good pay. work load. good place, but you have to find your work life balance. heavy workload on seasonal times. but good pay and annual bonuses. look out for some office politics. overall good experience for learning.

ok but a lot of challenges. generally the teams i worked in had nice people. if you work in the business support functions like marketing, procurement etc then expect to serve the business partners and consultants. being in a matrix organisation there are often competing priorities, unrealistic deadlines and a lot of politics to navigate. nearly all decisions are made by committee and it can be very hard to get everyone to agree. nothing very rarely happens quickly due to heavy internal processes as everything goes through risk teams for approval, this adds a lot of pressure to delivery functions. there ' s little freedom to be autonomous and do your job with freedom and there ' s constant restructures and redundancies. the company does have good employees benefits and initiatives like wellbeing days etc, but a lot of the things mentioned above contribute to a highly stressful environment and negative mental health. 70 % of my team had poor mental health or felt burned out and there ' s low morale across a lot of teams. the firm really needs to do more to improve the culture and the way it treats employees, we are more than just numbers on a sheet.
tough. long hours as expected but a great experience that you can take anywhere in the world. the majority of the staff were a joy to work alongside...........

okay place to work. while i struggle with finding proper individuals to be mentors to me and invest in my time here, the people i work with are usually pretty decent people and competent. promo and comp structure is pretty clear, though leadership can be somewhat unclear on goals

great place to learn. great place to start your career and learn a lot fast. your managers and your engagement teams make a big impact on your experience. it s very helpful to build a relationship with the partners on your team.

big four experience. kpmg was a good place to learn the mechanics of audit, but not always a great place to grow as a person. the training is solid and the clients are impressive, but the day - to - day reality often felt overwhelming and transactional. long hours, constant pressure to be billable, and understaffed teams made it hard to do quality work without burning out. managers varied wildly some were supportive and genuinely invested in development, while others treated associates as interchangeable. feedback was often rushed or inconsistent, and it could feel like survival mattered more than improvement. i m grateful for the technical foundation and the name on my r sum, but the culture made it difficult to feel valued or balanced. if you re looking for a fast, intense learning curve, it delivers just know it comes at a personal cost.

fast paced environment with opportunities to learn. working in the advisory practice there are a lot of opportunities to learn and work on different engagements with different clients. can be stressful at time and fast - paced. great benefits and pto.

as advertised. pros : - good training - exposure to large clients - learn a lot - looks good on a resume cons : - long hours aren t only in busy season anymore - micromanaged - lagging behind other b4 in salary

awful place to work. benefits. stressful burn - out favoritisme regional leader is unfair : - makes preferences based on religion and origins. - harrassement everyday to get clients and money. - writes to you at 11pm or midnigth to threaten you - uses inapropriate langage
hard to work for. no work - life balance there is a lot expected from you. i would not recommend a job here. thumbs down - a lot expected from you with no help from anyone

1. quickbooks 2. xero. a typical day at work involved bank reconciliations, transaction posting, invoicing, payroll support, and preparing reports using quickbooks and xero. i learned practical, hands - on bookkeeping, bank compliance requirements, and how to maintain accurate financial records. management was professional, supportive, and open to guidance, which encouraged learning and accountability. the workplace culture promoted teamwork, integrity, and attention to detail. the hardest part was managing multiple accounts under tight deadlines, while the most enjoyable part was solving discrepancies and delivering accurate financial insights.
overworked. you will be overworked, esp as a staff. be ready to put in atleast 70 hour weeks during busy season. not uncommon to work a few 90 hour weeks as well
good stepping stone. working life depends on luck. there are good and bad engagements, sustainable if you are lucky. overall perks are fair. but definitely there ' s somewhere else better.

negatives outweigh the positives. great work life balance but not very rewarding work. senior people do not care about junior staff development. people tend to have a corporate facade and are not very authentic.

great on resume. great for resume when starting out but managers and team members were not the best or very professional. experience can vary a lot by team. it s good when you re just starting your career
great. good. unclear promotion. good company to learn. got promoted to senior con fast but not to manager yet. difficult to get promotions if you are in the wrong team. but good work life balance.
good. good work life balance and pay. also employee friendly. learing is good. i thin kpmg is the best among when it comes to work life balance compared to other big 4s

big 4 - stepping stone for something else. it ' s large, as in huge. pros and cons present themselves accordingly. if you want to progress, you need to be great at navigating its politics. most competent young people, left before progressing substantially on their careers.
good place. i really enjoyed my time there. the firm culture is excellent and the people are really nice, especially in the consulting group, where everyone is really helpful.
wlb. long hours not a lot of pay.... can be rewarding depending on manager. great opportunity to have a lot of hands on learning in a short amount of time.

fun but demanding. kpmg is a great place to start your career as an entry level position. you meet so many people it feels like a natural progression after college. the first busy season is brutal 80 hour minimum weeks, no personal life, no vacation allowed. off - season is amazing but it ' s hard to find work - life balance.

very hierarchical. kpmg is not for free thinkers ; they are very hierarchical, discourage new ideas and extremely disempowering. senior staff esp, director and partner level are very condescending and look down on those with lower positions. that place will kill your innovative sprit if you have one
good work culture. good work place with great work culture. lots of opportunity to learn and network with other teams.

come to get the resume booster and experience than leave. overall it is very long hours with low pay. some really smart people and fortunate for me had some good bosses along the way. there are of course on occasion, some really bad clients and managers / partners but thats to be expected. go for the experience and resume booster. overall i ' m still glad i wen through the experience. even though it was tough.

decent experience. overall the experience was somewhat positive. hours are long and you are only focusing on a very small subset of what you would be at a smaller firm, where you would make more money
good place to learn. good place to learn and will pay for certifications. however entry level salary not competitive for large cities. people are pleasant and intelligent.

growth. work - life balance is hard to manage but still doable. however, the workload entails that you ' ll have to bring home some work from time to time. but the potential growth and ability to learn from working is there.
pretty good. had a wonderful time, it was great, and they provided a lot of learning opportunities. great place to start your career in accounting, accounting, and etc.

avoid. remote working once a week - that ' s the only good thing. all of them - remuneration package, hierarchy, toxic environment. avoid this company at all cost. they will exert you until you burn out and are forced out as soon as you don ' t comply with their demands. and if you don ' t, they will find something to report you on in their own internal assessment criteria.
long hours. long hours is expected for employees at all level and boring work. however, the culture and environment is fun and make it durable. structured with hiring process.

high turnover. the pace can be demanding, particularly during peak periods, but that environment accelerated my learning and forced me to become highly organized and detail - oriented. working closely with senior team members and clients also helped me develop the ability to translate complex financial analysis into clear, decision - relevant insights.

ideal for gaining foundational experience and learning on the job. not the best choice for a long - term career path. this position provides an excellent starting point for one s career, offering solid training and a relaxed work environment. it allows newcomers to gain valuable experience and develop essential skills. however, due to the nature of the work, overtime is unavoidable, which may limit its suitability for long - term career growth or advancement.

very solid. kpmg is a great place to work to gain experience into business management. they give you insights into pricing and managing clients at an earlier age than most companies. there is a lot of bueracracy and red tape with any audit firm and pay is just fine.
high pressure environment. some people are built for it but the hours are intense, and you are expected to be on any time of the day / night during busy season.

great people, very busy. the people are great. i enjoyed being around other professionals who are early in their careers, and they have great training programs. the busy season is just very tough, and really puts a strain on your well - being.

what you d expect. this job is exactly what you expect working in public accounting. lots of work in order to be successful, constantly being everything to everyone and can easily get overwhelming. that being said, the flexibility and benefits are unmatched and makes it hard to think about trying to find work elsewhere.

good first internship experience. it was interesting to get exposure and rotate through tax, advisory, audit. i did not feel like i had any chance to make meaningful contributions. my main focus was completing our internship project and presenting that to senior leaders. hybrid schedule was nice, but again did not allow for much hands on experience.

great opportunity for growth. i truly enjoy my director role at kpmg, where i lead high - performing teams, shape strategic initiatives, build strong client relationships, and drive measurable, lasting impact across complex engagements.

slavery without a railroad. work / life balance would be nice. it is mentioned a lot but only practiced by partners. tons of great folks to accompany you in the trenches, but be careful as some have already reached zombie status.
good to start your career. great place to start your career with multiple different opportunities. there is a definite push and pressure from above to keep costs low and charge clients high prices.

great place to grow and gain experience in the finance and accounting world. great training, strong team support, and exposure to many industries. it ' s a solid place to build skills and grow in accounting and finance. you will work a lot of overtime though!!!

not a fun place to be, but maybe good for experience long term? honestly, the experience you will have is going to be dependent on the teams you are put in. i think that some of the downtown audit groups were quite a bit better in terms of environment compared to the vaughan office.
fun and joyful and respectful. love the environment and work culture. been there for few years and really really enjoyed working in the team and have been treated with respect and good training.

remote option and good people. the work environment is collaborative and performance - driven, with good standards of quality and ethics. leadership is generally supportive, and the organization emphasizes integrity and continuous improvement. while peak seasons can demand long hours.

oddly unqualified mid - and top - level management. i worked for a bank it unit with team leads and project managers that had no basic understanding of it, who serviced customers who also had a poor understanding of it. total clusterfuck.
not fair. managers ' assessment for probation is not fair. huge workload, not like a human. cheap salary. co - workers support to complete tasks, not many events occur,

good working enviornment & people. kpmg is a good company for people who want to make career in statutory audit. it provides a good exposure in multiple sectors such as fmcg and financial sector. people working there are also professional and friendly.
productive and fun. very nice great beautiful good could be better but overall experience its fine and staff id very friendly and appreciate hard work withs gifts cards vouchers
very depress working environment. in a very depress working environment all you heard is keyboard sound lack of onboarding and training manager keep rush for progress pay is quite okay
ambitious and effective workplace. great colleagues, challenging work life balance, but worth it for the skills and experience gained. lovely london office and chance to work in cliemt spaces too

they don t hire the best. people are very nice and work / life balance is good. but that s it. they simply don t hire knowledgeable people. many of these people become upper management who create useless meetings to hide their lack of knowledge. the computers are bloated and slow which makes it very hard to do your work, costing the company time and money, which they seemed disinterested in fixing.

solid experience but demanding environment. working at kpmg provides strong exposure to complex clients and a good learning environment, especially early in your career. the training and brand recognition are definite advantages, and you work with smart, motivated colleagues. however, the workload can be intense during busy seasons, and work - life balance can be difficult to maintain. overall, it s a good place to build skills, but the pace and hours may not suit everyone long term.
good team. nice having these people in my team, however, resources are limited. much better if there are additional personnel for the engagements be equally divided properly.

good place to grow and work. a typical day at work involves collaborating with colleagues across departments, attending team meetings, and managing ongoing projects. the workload is steady and well - structured, allowing for both individual focus and teamwork. during my time here, i ve learned valuable skills in time management, communication, and problem - solving, as well as how to navigate a fast - paced, results - driven environment. management is approachable and generally supportive, offering guidance while giving employees room to take initiative. the workplace culture is inclusive and emphasizes continuous learning and collaboration, which makes it easy to feel part of a cohesive team. the hardest part of the job is balancing multiple priorities and meeting tight deadlines, especially during peak periods. however, the most enjoyable aspect is seeing projects come to life and being recognized for your contributions. overall, it s a rewarding place to work for anyone who values growth, teamwork, and meaningful professional experiences.

stressful work / life, good opps to learn. don t expect to work less than 50 hours / week, and there is a strong emphasis on data, artificial intelligence, and tech implementation. i went in for public health, and it s a hard time to be in that space. amazing colleagues if you can find a good project. isolating working from home / being removed from project site.
great place to work! i am part of deloitte consulting for more than 3 years know and still happy! lots of learning and growth opportunities, great colleagues and clients. : )
it was good. it was good working in deloitte, good projects and good salary lead to great work culture and people here are so talented and managaement is also good.

deloitte poor work culture and ethivs. meet on ms teams work hard and fast to meet daily quota very poor, all staff were to be trained in cultural sensitivity : by the demeaning way a project manager spoke down to he had neither cultural training or the tact to deal deal with & communicate with staff effectively. meeting the daily was the hardest we reviewed class actions docs that ranged from 8 to 80 pages. our quota was to average 3 per hr. this is sometimes impossible with claims that were longer in nature. the most enjoyable part if the job was working from home.

good paycheck but at a cost. almost at 4 years here and while there are great benefits ( flexibility, pto ) it comes with a ton of strings. i feel like my mental health has deteriorated over the years due to constant overwhelming pressure. it ' s not enough you do your job, to excel you need to be performing at three levels above you.
overworked. fun coworkers but unfortunately employees are continuously overworked despite cries for help. expected to do tasks outside of job description. not great pay.

no work life balance and very stressful. good benefits and learning opportunity. you will get great exposure. however, to succeed you have to give 100 % of your time, energy, and focus to the job. even then, you will fall short from what they will expect of you. toxic and stressful environment and would not recommend to anyone who wants to stay healthy, have a life outside of work, or be a part of their children s lives.
great career starter! perfect place to start to jumpstart your career to next level. it s tough for the first few years but the reward for all that hard work is worth it. there is always places to go
good. good company with exciting projects. hours a little bit stretched but overall you have good benefits and teams to work with. expect doing more that initially asked
overall good, but too much work. - lack of budget for certain projects, requiring you to overwork and stress - nice colleagues but bad team management desicions - good salary with bonus

high pressure and unorganized. working at deloitte was at one time rewarding. in the last few years, managers have been replaced by not sure what to call them with lots of turnover from people with actual skill & expertise to new well meaning folks with no real domain experience.
leadership. great overall. just be ready for the politics as usual. upskill always and know what s going on across the firm. live your values so you don t get controlled.
good to learn but no work / life balance. learn a lot ( by yourself ). no coaching, salary, too much work, toxic management. a lot expected from you but don t expect much from management

nice people, disorganized and weirdly run. i interned at deloitte one summer in one of the internal software teams, and it wasnt a well established intern program. took 3 weeks before i even got a project, and manager was very hands off to sometimes straight up ignoring me for several days. tech stack is dated, progress is slow.

internship. the ny office the people you work with are amazing ; it definitely depends on your engagement team. the engagement team i got was willing to teach even when busy. you can ask questions without feeling dumb. overall, the work environment is chill, and you have to do your work, but since it was summer probably not as stressful if the busy season.

experience is team dependent, good for new grads. it s a sink or swim situation, you need to learn to speak up and ask questions ( preferably compiled ) or else you ll be stuck spinning your wheels. be proactive!

stressful, fast paced environment. this was my first professional job after attending university. i still have nightmares about working there. it is a cut - throat working environment with incredible competition among workers. nice to have on resume, but i ' d hate to relive the experience.

good pay, poor market. gps aligned, currently difficult environment to work in unless you have a strong network. you will have to sell yourself to land a project and you will be going against highly qualified peers. if you can make it, the pay is good and deloitte is a good company to have in your resume.

great place to work. great place to work company values inclusion growth within the firm is highly competitive great work force yearly salary increase paid maternity leave deloitte days for rest and recharge

great place to learn and advance your career. career development, opportunities for learning, benefits. risk for burnout is always there. as part of the strategy and operations team, i was able to learn lots of different methodologies, functions and industries. opportunities for development are well aligned with skills develop, and there is always the chance to embark on new paths of learning and development. senior leadership is not only well aware on how to empower employees, and they also engage personally with resources to understand how to help them to prioritize projects and balancing out personal needs.

amazing jumpstart to career. skill development is insane and i feel like i ' ve grown leaps and bounds beyond my peers. however, work life balance is an immense challenge so navigating burnout is critical.

horrible place to work!! can t think of any. will shorten your life span because of the stress and anxiety. deloitte has probably the biggest revolving door. more and more people leave in short span of time then they join. biggest reason is the leadership. the unnecessary pressure trickles down from the top.

great learning opportunities. very great learning opportunities and exposure to leadership. handed lots of responsibility early on as well. however, the staffing model is strange as you often have to find projects yourself and cannot rely on your resource manager - creates for a sometimes stressful environment when you are not staffed / looking for new projects.
good place to start but not stay. pay is minimum, the work load is very much. leadership is not good. apart from that you are required to take care of nonsense and nobody is very supportive of u
management lies. it s bad place to work. honestly, you get typecasted into an industry and skill set and are not provided opportunities to grow. don t believe what the leadership says.

engaging and fast paced. good people if you find the right team, interesting work and fast paced environment. good benefits and great offices. i recommend working with as many people as possible to find the right team around you before you settle. easy to get silo into a niche expand into all areas to get breadth of experience. plenty of opportunities if you ask.
good for progression. great place to progress. would recommend someone to work here. is a great place to work and encourages progression within the company. lost of diversity within the workforce
long hours no balance. good people to learn from very knowledgable but sometimes hostile environments with demanding clients. too face paced and everything seems urgent even if it really isn t
retain talent. generous perks and strong culture of collegiality! folks who join tend not to leave and build long term careers at deloitte. work life balance is present as well
toxic. my manager is toxic. lack of growth and advancement. favoritism. however, still good pay and good work life balance no micro managing benefit is soso
friendly colleague. - senior willing to teach and very patient, can learn a lot and explore different industries - friendly colleagues, working with good people, good working culture

great for extroverts with technical skills. overall a great experience ; however, it was very difficult to find projects and meet utilization ratios. many projects were based on software implementations and had their own existing teams, very difficult to get into inner circles, too many new employees and not enough projects. constant fear of being let go.

the best. what is the best part of working at the company? great opportunity for growth, and benefits. what is the most stressful part about working at the company? searching for new projects after being hired. what is the work environment and culture like at the company? it is a culture that respect individuals as there are. what is a typical day like for you at the company? very engaging in projects and firm initiatives.
great place to work. great company, flexible work options, great benefits, lots of opportunity for growth and lots of training available for upskilling. people are very friendly.
it was fine. it was fine working here. obviously long stressful hours. learned a ton and set me up for success in my career. while i left on a good terms and made meaningful connections
flexibility. company gives flexibility. no micromanagement. long working hours in season. no transparency in comp revision or bonus. well being paid pda given in every two years.

typical day at deloitte. a typical day at deloitte is dynamic and fast - moving, often involving client meetings, project work, and collaboration with team members from different specialties. i learned how to think strategically, manage complex tasks, and communicate clearly in high - pressure environments. management is generally supportive and focused on growth, though expectations can be demanding. the workplace culture is professional, diverse, and built around teamwork, continuous learning, and innovation. the hardest part of the job is balancing tight deadlines and high workloads, especially during busy project cycles. the most enjoyable part is the exposure to interesting clients, meaningful challenges, and the chance to grow your skills quickly within a strong, reputable organization.

productive, precise, rewarding job. over productive to the point of hectic but great workplace. one can simply keep up with the work and have a good work - life balance provided the self discipline remains intact.

good work ethic, varied assignment. good balance of clients great colleagues good leaning environment, technology development was good, life work balance was fair due to the junior type of role involved, very few weekends worked

good for new grad. it ' s a good place to start ourtyour career after graduation if you ' re unsure what you want to do. however, you need to be careful on not getting stuck in an area you don ' t want to be in or else it ' ll be difficult to pivot later on.

good until it ' s not. extremely competitive environment, hard to trust your colleagues. i would suggest having a stipulated timeframe in which you learn as much as you can before seeking a healthier workplace. in essence it looks great on your cv.

the overall experience was ok. what is the best part of working at the company? deloitte provides excellent pay and benefits what is the most stressful part about working at the company? there is a high probability that they will have layoffs. what is the work environment and culture like at the company? excellent work environment as they allow both in office and hybrid alternatives.

grind. deloitte is widely regarded as one of the most influential professional services firms in the world, and my experience with the organization reflects why it consistently earns that reputation. the firm combines deep industry expertise with a culture that emphasizes continuous learning, collaboration, and high performance. what stands out immediately is deloitte s commitment to developing its people. whether through formal training programs, on - the - job coaching, or access to global knowledge resources, employees are continuously encouraged to expand their skills and take ownership of their professional growth. the work environment is fast - paced and dynamic, offering exposure to challenging projects across diverse industries. this variety fosters strong problem - solving abilities and adaptability, and it provides meaningful opportunities to work with high - caliber clients on issues that truly shape their businesses. deloitte s multidisciplinary model spanning audit, tax, consulting, financial advisory, and risk services also allows teams to collaborate cross - functionally, bringing well - rounded perspectives to complex client problems.

great opportunity. team work. odd working hrs, 11 - 8, prefers early working than late hrs. i had a great opportunity to work with excellent management and collaborate with numerous managers across various projects. this experience provided me with valuable learning opportunities and allowed me to grow both professionally and personally. i truly enjoyed working with my team and appreciated the supportive and dynamic work environment.

pretty good. fun, nice workplace community. pay was low. ye loved it. showed up in the morning, walked around and just did my work. work culture was really nice. everyone was nice to eachother and made eachother feel valued.
less pay more workload. kind of a good company to start with however pay isn t good and too much workload. it can be a good place to work in 5 years if you are ready to wait that long

very good company with competitive compensation and very good benefits. challenging and fast - paced working environment with opportunities for growth and training. like most professional service firms, you are at the whim of the partner in charge of your respective area. most are very smart and reasonable with expectations, but overall work life balance is heavily weighted towards work which then transforms into the norm.
excellent. excellent experience at deloitte, management is welcoming and a lot of ressource to train, i would see it ' s in my top 3 jobs of all time. recommand it very much
not for me. no projects lack of people management toxic environment from management no worklife balance no structure and responsibility only positive point is 5 / 5 smart working
great firm. overall great place to work. wonderful benefits, smart colleagues, great resources. recommend to anyone looking for a flexible and enjoyable career.

lots of opportunities but little work / life balance. there are many opportunities to work on local, national, and international clients of all kinds and profiles. people are generally supportive of teaching you and helping you learn, including making accommodations so you can attend trainings. however, i am not happy about the work / life balance. in consulting, the minimum amount of client hours you have to work is 45 hours / week. that ' s the bare minimum. not to mention, you have to juggle working on internal initiatives and 40 hours of training per year on top of that. and leadership normalizes all that work. you have to hope you have a manager that respects your work - life balance because it really depends on the project you are on.

learning. working at deloitte can be both challenging and rewarding, but it often feels like being back in school due to the heavy emphasis on structure and compliance. compliance is taken very seriously, and even minor issues such as missing a timesheet can result in penalties. the culture tends to lean toward micromanagement, and advancement requires keeping multiple stakeholders satisfied, which can be difficult unless you are ready to consistently work 12 15 hours a day. although there is no overtime compensation, employees are still generally expected to work more than 50 hours per week. on the positive side, there is an annual training opportunity at deloitte university ( du ), which is a valuable experience. however, overall, the workload and expectations can be intense
solid company. great benefits ( during covid ). however, most of the teammates are pretty type - a. make sure you align with the culture, otherwise you might be pushed out.

great plce to work. great management. some micromanaging leaders. many growth opportunities. learning. personal development. it is a great place to work and achieve your dreams and ambitions. management always supports your opinions.

work 24 / 7. benefits are great when you can use them. no teamwork. lack of people with skills to staff projects. teams are 1 onshore and rest offshore. leadership is extremely inconsistent between projects. teams work in silos and rarely collaborate even when the other team has the requirements. spend 9 hours in meetings for clients, to work a few hours on sales pursuits, and then meeting with offshore team before going to bed. typically, min of 40 hours of client work and then 10 - 20 % ( required for a good rating ) working on developing the consulting practice.

chill intern experience. received a return offer but didnt take it. generally easy going environment with supportive network for interns. poor pay but its big 4 so expected. nice building and lots of desks - there is fruit and a few snacks in the office but not too many

very supportive and flexible regarding location change, wfh. just like any other consulting firm, ofcourse the work pressure is very high and the work timings are really long, but it provides good learning and exposure. timings are flexible.

challenging and growth environment. has a bright future within the firm. their passion for tackling the most challenging it problems while maintaining a truly fun and energetic team dynamic makes them an indispensable leader. we look forward to seeing them take on a principal consultant role in the near future, leveraging their strategic influence across an even wider portfolio of clients

productive. a top - tier firm offering unparalleled experience, networking, and a strong resume brand. however, the intense hours and work - life balance issues are a major drawback. not for everyone.

friendly seniors and coaching. i completed an internship at deloitte and found it to be a valuable and formative experience. the firm provides strong exposure to real client work and allows interns to better understand how professional services operate in practice. during my time there, i was involved in a variety of tasks that helped me develop both technical skills and professional discipline, including documentation review, basic analysis, and supporting ongoing engagements. one of the key strengths of deloitte is its structured working environment. there are clear processes, expectations, and quality standards, which helped me learn how to manage deadlines and communicate effectively within a team. colleagues were generally professional and supportive, and i had opportunities to ask questions and learn through hands - on experience rather than purely theoretical training. the workload could be demanding at times, especially when deadlines approached, but this also reflected the realities of the industry and helped me build resilience and time - management skills. overall, the internship provided meaningful insight into the accounting and advisory profession and was a solid foundation for anyone considering a career in this field.

flexible and accommodating. had a good environment to work in, once you pass 1 year you are obligated to go in only 4 times a month. work load varies a lot just depends on what partner you get assigned

unwanted pressure. not all partners. specialy in group 1. they have high expectations which can not be filled with juniors they are not properly trained. other than that small mistakes are taken seriously in fs like space and commo

good variety of experience but huge workload. you go from project to project and your role can change completely and the hours change with this... although this can be interesting to try new things, it ' s often hard to specialize when you do a lot of roles.
decent overall. decent overlap, not too much innovation which is expected for the industry. some projects can be channelling while mostly is the same work applied in different aspects
good internship experience. despite entering during the busy season, it was somehow bearable. the colleagues are approachable whenever i have questions about a task for an engagement.

professional work space. professional work space, fun events held often, good benefits! would recommend it to my friends! you need to pay attention when you choose your project through, some are demanding but rewarding while other are not!
fast pace culture. fast paced culture and you need to be able to keep up. this includes working outside the normal 40. team depends on who ur assigned to but can make or break ur experiences.

great place to work. great place to work. no wlb. the yearly hikes are good and above market standards. also, perks offered by the company is good such as mobile allowance, well being subsidies and other benefits.

great place to work - but low pay and progression. the role can be interesting and is really a mix of finance and administrative work. more administrative than you think, so not too heavy on finance and data analytics. very good work life balance but the pay falls behind other financial roles. that and career progression can be difficult with roles cemented but that means good expertise and processes for easy transitions into the job. culture is really great as it can be for remote.
intense, fun. can be intense at times, but there is a strong passion for growth and team collaboration. highly recommend working at deloitte and gaining experience of the best big 4.
good. learning and wlb depends on the project and team. overall good with benefits and perks. they have a lot of good projects in pipeline however one should be open to travel

tick tok. they hire you, and once you reach a certain pay rate, they fire you. that ' s the culture there. the people you work with are good, but leadership will let you go as soon as you reach a certain pay rate and replace you with someone new at a lower rate.

fun place. my experience at deloitte has been both challenging and incredibly rewarding. as part of global teams, i ' ve worked on diverse projects solving real - world business problems for clients across various industries. the culture emphasizes collaboration, continuous learning, and professional growth, with ample training and mentorship opportunities. while the pace is fast and demands are high, the sense of accomplishment and the skills gained are immense. deloitte provides a dynamic environment where innovation is encouraged, allowing you to build a robust network and a strong foundation for your career. it s a place that truly pushes you to grow and excel.

wonderful job. benefits can ' t be beat. handle time. a typical day at work for me consisted of taking anywhere from 100 - 200 calls assisting customers with not only their credit card accounts but their retail accounts as well. i did everything from processing pymnts, closing accts, reducing and increasing credit lines, redeeming rewards, getting them to chase travel, assisting them with app and website navigation, adding and removing cards from digital wallets. on the retail side i assisted with ordering new debit cards, increasing and decreasing daily purchase limits along with atm limits. customers would sometimes call in to open, close, add or reduce the amount of funds they had in their cd ' s. i learned a lot about banking all together dealing with these accounts and can say i got a lot of joy from it. it also made me think outside the box to help come up with solutions that some didn ' t think of. the management was wonderful, in my time there i had a total of 8 and that may seem like a lot to some, but i was there for almost 12 years, moved departments ( me and the managers sometimes went on projects ), schedule changes. i worked in office for my first 2 - - the amount of socialising and a strong pressure to do it - they didn ' t follow my agency contract and i had to do more days in the office than my contract said - 3 redundancies in 2 years time ( 2 general ones that were announced just before christmas and 1 just for my agency team ) - lack of stability ( contracts extended every 6 - 12 months ) - lack of opportunities for me even though i said what i wanted to do - i lost my job, because i had a career goal in another department. my workflow was excellent, but they still didn ' t extend my contract. they hired a new person instead while i was still there. - hiring data analysts from india, because it ' s cheaper, even though there are so many skilled people in the uk who can ' t find jobs - i had to travel once a quarter for a team meeting and stay in a hotel ( it wasn ' t optional ) - men got better career opportunities than women. they also got more side projects and networking. - my 2 days secondment in another department was declined. so was my 1 day coaching course that i was invited for - low pay for agency employees ( below barclays salary brackets for this job level if you are hired through an agency ) - not considering agency employees job applications for the positions with barclays contracts what is the work environment and culture like at the company? toxic. you always have to smile and be corporate positive, regardless of how you feel. if you ' re sad, you can get a negative feedback that may affect your job. very strong pressure to socialise and take part in all team meetings. demanding more skills than the role requires, such as presenting and public speech, even if you are just an admin. what is a typical day like for you at the company? overworked. many jobs today demand more than they compensate, but few exemplify this imbalance as clearly as roles in customer support, healthcare, retail, and it service management. these positions often require long hours, immense mental and emotional effort, and the ability to juggle multiple responsibilities, all while offering salaries that barely reflect the workload. workload : beyond reasonable expectations overworked employees frequently find themselves covering tasks meant for multiple people. in industries like it service management, for example, professionals are expected to maintain critical systems, manage stakeholder expectations, and ensure seamless service delivery all with limited resources and tight deadlines. similarly, nurses and retail workers endure exhausting shifts, often staying beyond their scheduled hours without overtime pay. compensation : a disappointing reality despite the demanding nature of these jobs, the financial rewards rarely match the effort. many companies set salary caps that do not align with inflation, the rising cost of living, or the specialized skills required for the role. bonuses and raises, if offered, are minimal, and in some cases, employees are expected to take on additional responsibilities without extra pay. work - life balance : nonexistent one of the biggest drawbacks of overworked and underpaid jobs is the lack of work - life balance. employees are constantly on call, forced to work weekends, or pressured to meet unrealistic deadlines. the stress of the job often spills into personal life, leading to burnout, mental health struggles, and a declining quality of life. lack of recognition and career growth another frustrating aspect is the lack of appreciation from employers. hard work is often met with more responsibilities rather than rewards or promotions. many employees find themselves stuck in a cycle of endless work with no clear career progression. conclusion : a broken system overworked and underpaid jobs create an unsustainable work environment. while passion and dedication keep employees going, financial security and fair treatment are essential for long - term satisfaction. companies must recognize that investing in their workforce both financially and emotionally leads to better productivity, lower turnover rates, and a healthier work culture. until that happens, employees in these roles will continue to struggle, making these jobs some of the most frustrating and unrewarding in the workforce.

good. working at barclays center is a rewarding and exciting experience. as one of the premier sports and entertainment venues in the world, it offers employees the chance to be part of high - profile events, including nba games, concerts, and major cultural showcases. the fast - paced, energetic environment fosters a strong sense of teamwork and camaraderie. employees often take pride in helping deliver top - tier guest experiences, whether through hospitality, security, or event operations. barclays center also emphasizes growth and development, offering training programs and advancement opportunities for motivated individuals. the diversity of the staff and the inclusive culture make it a welcoming place to work, where different backgrounds and talents are valued. plus, working behind the scenes at iconic events creates unforgettable memories and a real sense of being part of something big. for those passionate about entertainment, sports, and service, barclays center is an exciting place to build a career.

results - oriented. working at barclays as a business systems analyst has been a professionally enriching experience. a typical day involves gathering requirements from stakeholders, analyzing existing systems, documenting functional specifications, and working closely with developers and testers to ensure seamless project execution. daily stand - ups and stakeholder meetings are common, especially in agile environments. i ve learned a lot about enterprise banking systems, cross - functional collaboration, and the importance of aligning technology with business strategy. the role has also enhanced my problem - solving and communication skills. management is generally supportive and encourages professional growth. they promote autonomy while being available for guidance when needed. the workplace culture is inclusive, diverse, and collaborative. there ' s a strong emphasis on continuous learning and innovation, and colleagues are approachable and helpful. the hardest part of the job is managing multiple high - priority projects with tight deadlines, especially when requirements change frequently. the most enjoyable part is seeing your contributions translate into impactful system improvements, along with the satisfaction of solving complex business challenges.

chill and productive workplace. working at barclays has been a positive and rewarding experience. the company places a strong emphasis on employee well - being and offers an excellent work - life balance. managers are supportive, and there s a healthy culture of collaboration and mutual respect across teams. barclays also provides opportunities for professional development and encourages continuous learning. the work environment is dynamic yet manageable, making it an ideal place for those who value both career growth and personal time. overall, barclays is a great place to work, especially for those who are looking for a balanced and supportive workplace.

strange experience, while outwardly pleasant there are undertones of forced conformity and broad compliance that inhibits growth and innovation. what is the best part of working at the company? great team to work with, especially in the us. great collaboration and teamwork what is the most stressful part about working at the company? lack of clarity of the role, especially upon hire. job description is intentionally vague due to the constant changing of properties as new leadership tasks over. minimal training, minimal time or opportunity to learn the culture and structure of the organization before being rushed to perform. what is the work environment and culture like at the company? very hierarchical organization with little allowance for autonomy without pre - approval of every aspect of the role. leadership plays favorites and rewards compliance while preaching the importance of speaking up. there is also an undertone of arrogance from leadership across the bank that promotes fear from lower level employees to challenge the status quo out of fear of retaliation or being black balled for potentially saying the wrong thing to the wrong audience.
great benefits and environment. the benefits were awesome and the office was nice and well kept. was recently renovated as well. managers were always supportive.

can be a good place to work. looked after, respected, good company to be employed by. bad management decisions. at barclays there was a huge focus on the individual and work life balance and mental health was utmost priority. whilst i enjoyed my time here i found that some of the processes were outdated and could have been streamlined ( i was making outbound calls asking for security before i could state the reason for calling, to high end business clients which wasrang alarm bells for fraud ) and at the time we worked from outlook so i found that i was doing a lot of the work that others on the team could not be bothered too. along side this covid hit and all other teams were starting to work from home but we were told this was not allowed by my managers manager, after a conversation with hr this was not at all the case so following this let down i decided to part ways for a new career.

covid ruined the bank. work colleagues, hours. pay. i really enjoyed my job working here and enjoyed actually going to work. untill covid hit, turned into a call centre,. lost the customer facing role after all of that. asking to work from home when wasn t able to

poor place to work. in my personal experience, working at barclays in northampton has been extremely disappointing and far from what i expected from a major employer. the environment is stressful and overly micromanaged, with little room for individuality or input. managers often focus more on hitting unrealistic kpis than actually supporting their teams. i found myself constantly under pressure, with very little recognition for the work i put in. it felt like no matter how hard you worked, it was never enough. career development is practically non - existent unless you ' re part of the inner circle. favouritism plays a huge role in who gets noticed or promoted, and those who speak up or challenge poor practices are often overlooked. i went in hoping for growth and progression, but quickly realised it was a dead end.

pay is excellent. working at barclays had many benefits, especially in terms of compensation. the pay was competitive, and the bonuses were generous and consistent, which made the role financially rewarding. the company offered solid training and resources, and the overall day - to - day work environment was fast - paced yet supportive. however, a major downside was the unexpected mass layoff of over 300 employees, which came without prior notice or clear communication. the sudden nature of the decision was disappointing and created a sense of instability among remaining staff. while the experience and compensation were valuable, the abrupt layoffs left many feeling undervalued and uncertain about long - term job security.
just ok. good benefits but workload can be stressful at times, micromanaging is also an issue and can feel like a call centre culture, hybrid working is good though

good pay, stressful work. the pay is good, the management is supportive as they can possibly be and they have great benefits and team time but the training is based on values and ethos rather than what you need to be taught to service the calls, so each call is a rush of panic and anxiety trying to navigate the solution for the problem. you re monitored on every single detail of every call. how long it takes on the call, the after call time for taking notes, how long the customers on hold and other details of the call which adds a lot of pressure on top of the general servicing. the shifts vary depending on what rota you re put on ( no choice from beginning ) you re either on 4 in 4 off 10 - 12 hour shifts from 8am - 11pm or 5 days a week ranging from 7am - 7pm.

amazing experience with barclays, would definitely recommend! compensation, learning / experience, career growth. work / life balance. worked across sales & trading at barclays, helping drive client growth strategy. fast - paced, collaborative, and a great place to learn how large - scale financial systems really operate.

it was good while it lasted. the employees are all well - meaning and work hard together for an absolutely complete and successful outcome, even collaborating when needing improvement or a problem arises. i was on the best team for my region and they still gave our jobs to india, so the big wigs don ' t care ultimately. plus, it ' s global based out of the uk so the us portion didn ' t matter.

a great learning environment. i worked at barclays for more than 3 years as a senior ux designer. we were working in hybrid mode with 8 - 10 hours of work every day. our team was extremely qualified in ux, we had a good work - life balance, and we had a friendly work culture. i got a chance to do good user research, mentor junior designers, and handle multiple projects at the same time with high dependency on the ux side.

staff morale is declining. barclays has seen better days in terms of staff morale and fairness. if you re looking for growth, recognition, or job security, you may find yourself disappointed in the current environment. the best thing about the job were my colleagues and you can gain some valuable experience to progress your career ( elsewhere ). internal promotions are often pre - determined, with management seemingly already aware of who will get the role before it ' s advertised. a significant portion of roles are being moved to india, leading to redundancies in the uk. the remaining uk - based staff are expected to take on additional responsibilities to cover the shortfall, often without appropriate recognition or compensation. bonuses were once a real incentive, but over the last few years they ve become minimal and no longer reflect performance or contribution. in your yearly review most employees receive the same performance rating regardless of their effort or results. unless you ' re a manager s favourite or in trouble, there s little differentiation or transparency in the process.
productive. pros - good pay and compensation good environment cab facilities good infrastructure cons - poor management work - life balance have to visit office twice weekly internal politics

intellectually stimulating but environment matters. great mentors and learning opportunities but finance in nyc is a different culture than in other cities. it is a place where people live in the city because they either a ) love the urban density and diversity or b ) their career means everything.

great company. i started my career in the branch in a temporary customer service role and have since progressed over the years into project management - barclays offer great internal mobility and opportunities to grow your career. they also offer the opportunity to apply for educational funding of courses and have fantastic apprentice / grad schemes decent pay that is yearly reviewed and really good benefits with share purchase options and a good work / life balance inclusive, laid back campus and great amenities - i would recommend anyone thinking of applying here to do it

great place to learn. barclays bank offers a structured and professional environment for qa analysts. the role involves ensuring that systems and processes meet high standards of quality, with a strong focus on compliance, risk management, and attention to detail. the position provides insight into the operations of a major financial institution, with exposure to complex business workflows and regulatory requirements. collaboration is a key part of the role, with frequent interaction between quality assurance, development, and business teams. the organization maintains clear processes and expectations, which supports consistent delivery and accountability. working at barclays can be fast - paced and demanding, but it also presents opportunities to develop a deep understanding of financial services and quality governance in large - scale environments. the company emphasizes continuous improvement and encourages professional development through internal learning resources and structured feedback. overall, it s a solid experience for qa professionals seeking growth in a well - established, global organization.

working environment. working area is clean and nice to new commer. small groups but they have good quality. they are very friendly, making small and large components for mining equipment, full - time opportunity for new commer, large production area good behavior weekly pay
good workplace culture. good employee benefits and inclusive environment to work in. mgmt was encouraging and promote open communication amongst teams. diversity in teams and peers.
great workplace. working there was a great time. i rarely had to go into the office. office is beautiful so i never minded. overall great place to work. pay is decent as well. i would go back.

great interview experience. was interviewed by an analyst and associate. great interview, the questions weren ' t hard and the interviewers were encouraging and very kind. much nicer experience than other investment bank interviews.

good job. working at barclays has been an enriching experience that combines professional growth with a collaborative work environment. the company fosters a strong culture of learning, offering training programs and resources that help employees enhance both technical and soft skills. leadership is approachable and supportive, encouraging open communication and innovative thinking. teamwork is highly valued, and there s a sense of camaraderie across departments, which makes tackling challenges more manageable and rewarding. the work itself is engaging, with opportunities to contribute to meaningful projects that impact both clients and the wider financial community. while the workload can be demanding at times, it comes with ample opportunities for career advancement and skill development. barclays also prioritizes diversity, inclusion, and employee well - being, which contributes to a positive and motivating atmosphere. overall, it s a company that balances professional challenges with personal growth, making it an excellent place to build a career in finance.
good company. progressive and fun with opportunities and job stability. good fringe benefits. regular performance reviews. regular training and feedback. innovative

dedicated. a typical day in this role involves managing a variety of client queries across business banking and, occasionally, personal, premier, and tesco retail accounts. the work requires strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and excellent communication skills to resolve complex issues efficiently while ensuring compliance and a positive client experience. teamwork is central to the role, as colleagues collaborate to manage workloads, share knowledge, and maintain consistent service standards. the environment provides valuable opportunities for learning and skill development, including exposure to different client segments, problem - solving, and operational processes. management is supportive and approachable, offering guidance, feedback, and training to help employees succeed. open communication ensures that staff feel recognized and empowered to contribute ideas or seek assistance when needed. challenges include balancing multiple priorities and resolving complex client issues under tight deadlines, which helps build resilience, adaptability, and confidence. the most rewarding aspects are assisting clients effectively, developing expertise across various banking areas, and contributing to team success. overall, the role fosters professional growth, skill development, and a client - focused mindset in a collaborative and supportive environment.

great salary and work environment. up - to - date technology, very challenging tasks and a lot to learn. i worked as a member of a development team and i have good experience. you will never be bored!

ok. good chances to progress. tough targets and active management out of staff at times. regular restructures. investment in working environments and acceptance of diversity

great company. i greatly enjoyed my time at barclays. i started as a front - line csr and was able to work my way up through the company to a training role. i would still be with barclays if they had not shut down operations in ohio.

easy work. being a sales apprentice at barclays has been an incredibly rewarding experience that combines hands - on learning with professional development. from day one, i felt supported by a strong network of mentors and colleagues who are genuinely invested in my growth. the structured training programme gave me a clear understanding of barclays products, services, and values, while also helping me build practical sales skills like relationship management, communication, and problem - solving.

the most enjoyable part of my job and got lot of experience. i feel more energetic most of experience i gain from there one of best tome to work with barclays. i recommend new joiner to go and join with them. thanks

depends on team. like with most large banks, your experience is highly dependent on your manager and team. i ve moved internally twice and 2 out of 3 of those teams were toxic. though i appreciate that the second toxic team i joined, my manager really tried to shield me from the politics.

good workplace. good workplace with too many rules to make excellent customer service. the times that a client can drop in and get help is over. nothing is going without an appointment and waiting times.

productive, professional company. worked for barclays for many years in various rolls from call centre environment to face to face in branch. lots of career progression and good company to work for.
good. great business to work for when you needed time off they respected that they really looked after you as a business great sense of feeling when helping clients

genuinely awful. impossible to get working hours, management aren t particularly helpful even when asked for help, drama constantly at work ( management included ). management can also be very disrespectful

stressed and rushed. remote working. the stress, the pace, and accurate note - keeping whilst on the calls / chats is very hard, didn ' t get support requested in my reviews. poor management. taking calls and chats ( 2 at a time ) and answering queries. with 2 chats at a time, the system would need you to copy and paste contact details for id & v, but the system would show the wrong name in the chat window. you need to be hyper vigilant as it would be easy to respond to the wrong chat box ( sometimes you would have 4 windows for 2 chats. the policy / procedure changes constantly, and little support is available. if you miss the team meeting ( most newcomers would as we ' d be stuck on chats ) my manager wouldn ' t update me on the changes. they say flexible breaks, but if you take them as such, they complain that you have a break queued. i was so stressed working there, it made me ill. i ' m so glad not to be working there anymore.

pays well but there s no advancement. higher than average salary. boring, dead end position. they re ableist and will go out of their way to block advancement if you re disabled. they make very little adjustments, if any. the company pays lip service to staff mental health and do not care about staff safety at all.
fun workplace. it s a fun workplace to be in, manageable work loads depending on your team management. very many inefficiencies in tech and decision making - but that s pretty much everywhere.

horrible career progression and no wlb. could be just my team - there ' s little wlb here oncall comes mostly every month and your colleague just feels free to call you anytime after work or on holiday. there is no career progression unless you are in exceptionally good terms with your boss. join somewhere else if you can.

great place to work. pay, employee perks like saye, share purchase scheme. systems let us down on many occassions, could do with upgrading to latest tech. i loved my time at barclays, great place to work and really looks after you as an employee. management is really supportive and had a great experience.

great place to work. i got nothing wrong to say about barclays, they value their staff, good salary and great perks. i have been working there for over 12 years and no complaints from me. may be the best financial place to work with.

relaxed summer technology internship. the work load is very low and there is very little pressure. the return offer is almost guaranteed, which we soon found out, which meant we weren ' t working so hard as a cohort. there are relatively clever employees, and nice benefits. it is an awkward place to commute to, which can be a pain. the actual work that we did was quite interesting, but it really varies from team to team. some teams were not very technical. there is lots of support and lots of opportunities for growth. the pay is good, especially considering we were working < 30 hours / week.

enjoyed the job. it at radbroke hall, great place to work, excellent facilities, great food, on site parking, lovely staff, on site gym with classes, well maintained beautiful hall with corporate external buildings

great learning experience with strong technical exposure. strong mentorship, exposure to enterprise systems, structured agile workflow, good learning opportunities. complex systems can have a steep learning curve ; processes can be highly structured. a typical day involved working on backend apis using java and spring boot, assisting with frontend enhancements, and collaborating with senior developers during agile stand - ups. i learned about enterprise - level application architecture, secure coding practices, and how large financial systems are designed and maintained. management was supportive and encouraged learning. the workplace culture was professional and collaborative. the hardest part was understanding complex legacy systems, while the most enjoyable part was contributing to real production features and seeing them deployed.

ai generated. my time as an investment banking analyst at barclays was intense, fast - paced, and transformative. i developed strong financial modeling and valuation skills while working on complex, high - stakes transactions. the long hours pushed my resilience, but the collaborative culture and exposure to senior bankers accelerated my professional growth.

targets, targets, targets. decent pay but not worth the stress of the job. the shifts feel so long and slow you find yourself clock - watching all day. there is constant pressure of meeting targets, banks aren ' t supposed to have sales targets anymore, but they absolutely do still expect you to promote products etc and just use different wording to get away with it. you will end up on performance improvement places and given disciplinaries and eventually fired if you don ' t have enough " conversations " about products with customers. you are watched very carefully to make sure you spend every last possible second with your headset on and taking calls to the point that even how long you spend going to the toilet for a " comfort break " is scrutinised. essentially, there is not much empathy from leaders and higher ups for staff wellbeing, especially mentally, and the job is all about hitting targets.
every day different. not bad but a lot of sugar coating around sales targets became gruelling. good teamwork in place but a lot expected from staff too. often expected to turn up early and stay late.

good benefits but very mentally demanding. would avoid. pension. more or less everything. very long review but i want to make it clear to people who are about the make the same mistake as i did. as somebody who has never worked in a call centre before, this job was the most mentally draining position i have ever faced. you are sat at a computer for 6 hours a day with a headset on taking calls, and you are tracked from the minute your shift starts until the minute it ends. they want you taking calls constantly. your after call work is monitored and you are expected to be in it for around 20 seconds, so tough luck if you didn t get all your notes completed on the call or if you need to raise something for a customer once the call has finished. if you are in it after call work for any longer, your team leader will physically change your status back to ready. your average call handling time is monitored and you will be nagged for it, despite management knowing every call is different. even checking your own stats is monitored. the customers are what really ruin this job. you will speak to some really nasty people every single day. demanding things that cannot be done, arguing with you, guilt tripping you, trying to make you feel bad for them, and speaking to you however they want. you just have to sit there and take it. you cannot end the call either, you have to wait for them to end it. so a lot of the time you will be stuck on calls for 20 + minutes repeating the same information until they finally decide to give up. the bank doesn t help as they have a lot of silly processes and some of the decisions they make make the job so much harder, and these decisions are made by people who have most likely never worked on the phones in their life and will never have to face any customers their decisions affect. the biggest example of this is how they close all the branches, which makes it very difficult when dealing with elderly or vulnerable customers. sometimes the only way an issue can be resolved is by the customer needing to visit a branch, but when they can t and there is no other option, you are still expected to ask them what other support they need to help with their banking, when they are already complaining the support they receive is not good enough from us. you have to force yourself through the conversation and it s honestly embarrassing, but if you don t discuss it, qa will fail your call and you will be in the wrong for not providing

good benefits cheap health insurance. great benefits free coffee generous pto. favoritism. the health insurance is really affordable, especially for a family. the lunch room has a cafe where you can order hot food. the company caters on a regular basis, so free lunches for employees. also, you get free coffee! they also have events where you can bring your spouse and kids sometimes. one down side is the favoritism. a supervisor might spend 3 hours talking to their younger opposite gender subordinates on company time. upper management doesn ' t care as long as someone does the work. so you might have a coworker that takes extra long lunch breaks and spends excessive time with your leader while you do all the work. you probably won ' t get thanked by that supervisor for all that work you did. they will probably nitpick and micromanage you so that you feel small and unimportant. the atmosphere is very positive! everyone is really nice. the retention rate is astronomically low, like your yearly raises. there is plenty of growth opportunities and room for advancement. it is very easy to get promoted at abbott as long as you sabotage your coworkers and make friends with the right people. everyone likes to get to know each other on a personal level. some supervisors make it their entire job to talk to people their entire shift. you don ' t have to worry about the constraints of getting your work done when you pass it to the next shift. the pto is generous and easy to use. you ' ll probably need to take many mental health days so it ' s really convenient.

it was a fast - paced and sometimes demanding environment, but it taught me resilience, adaptability, and the importance of teamwork. what is the best part of working at the company? the work life balance, the good leadership and understanding management what is the most stressful part about working at the company? the most stressful part of working in the company is handling multiple tasks under strict deadlines while ensuring accuracy. since we deal with sensitive data and client concerns, even small errors can have big consequences. on top of that, managing client expectations, addressing urgent escalations, and sometimes working on shifting schedules can be very demanding. balancing quality, speed, and customer satisfaction is really the biggest challenge. what is the work environment and culture like at the company? the work environment is fast - paced and detail - oriented, which can be challenging but also very rewarding. the culture encourages teamwork and accountability, so everyone is expected to deliver their part while supporting each other. i ve learned to adapt quickly and stay focused even under pressure. what is a typical day like for you at the company? a typical day for me starts with checking emails and pending tasks to prioritize what needs immediate attention. most of my day is spent handling billing concerns, updating records, creating accounts for medical personnel, and responding to client or clinic inquiries. i also make sure to document issues properly and coordinate with the team if there are escalations. it s a mix of accuracy, communication, and customer support throughout the day.

innovative, collaborative, and employee - centric environment. with 12 years of industry experience and 3 years at novartis, i can confidently say that this company stands out for its commitment to innovation and collaboration. novartis offers a dynamic environment where employees are encouraged to push boundaries, and the leadership is deeply invested in our growth and well - being. pros : cutting - edge innovation : novartis is at the forefront of pharmaceutical r & d, always pushing for advancements that truly make a difference in patients ' lives. being part of this mission is fulfilling and inspiring. collaborative culture : teamwork and cross - functional collaboration are highly emphasized here. this has allowed me to build strong relationships across various departments, which enriches the work experience and leads to more successful outcomes. work - life balance : novartis offers flexibility that promotes a healthy work - life balance, with options for remote work and support for personal development. this balance is crucial for someone like me, juggling both professional and personal responsibilities. career development : with numerous learning and development programs, i ve been able to continuously upgrade my skills. internal mobility is encouraged, and there are clear paths for career growth for those who seek it. diversity and inclusion : novartis has a strong commitment to creating an inclusive environment, with many initiatives supporting diversity across gender, ethnicity, and regional representation. this diversity is reflected not only in the workforce but also in the perspectives that shape our projects. cons : large organization complexity : given the size of novartis, navigating internal processes can sometimes be challenging. it may take time to get decisions approved, which can slow down certain projects. fast - paced environment : while the fast pace can be exhilarating, it sometimes feels demanding, especially when juggling multiple priorities. however, the company does provide plenty of resources to help manage workloads and avoid burnout. overall : in my 3 years at novartis, i ve experienced tremendous growth and have been proud to contribute to projects that truly impact global health. novartis is a fantastic place for anyone looking to be part of a purpose - driven organization that values innovation, collaboration, and its people. it s a company where you can make a difference and build a long - term, fulfilling career.

work - life balance, great place for career progression. working at novartis has been a rewarding experience, marked by a strong emphasis on flexibility, growth, and global opportunities. one of the standout benefits is the hybrid working model, which allows employees to maintain a healthy work - life balance. the company also supports flexible working hours, which is especially valuable for managing personal commitments alongside professional responsibilities. novartis fosters a culture of internal mobility, offering employees the opportunity to explore roles in different countries through internal job postings. this global exposure not only broadens professional horizons but also enriches personal development. one challenge, however, is the frequent organizational changes and minor restructuring. while these shifts are often aimed at improving efficiency and alignment with strategic goals, they can sometimes create uncertainty or require quick adaptation. overall, novartis provides a supportive and forward - thinking work environment that values both employee well - being and career progression.
