great experience. all expenses paid party. unsociable working hours. i thoroughly enjoyed my position within this renowned company. i achieved and often exceeded all set targets and the company responded warmly with praise and merits. this helped form an excellent relationship between employer and employee and much mutual respect was apparent. i learned to utilise social media in such a way that would effectively promote and advertise the company. my co - workers were fantastically supportive and helpful at the beginning of my role and i enjoyed the entire experience, including the expenses - paid party at the end of our contracts. i didn ' t find the role difficult as help was always available and targets were always easily achievable with hard work and dedication.
great experience. i made sure that the guards that were on the shift with me were moving around and making sure no one entered into the work space of spotify. i learned a lot
great company! such a great company to work for! the work life balance is phenomenal and there are plenty of perks. such as free catered lunches, free concerts and company events.

neat gig for students. free stuff, eligible cash benefits. short length, not enough physical interaction ( imo ). great experience for a college student looking to network or get experience in that kind of position. we got students pumped about spotify and had a blast doing it.

amazing. it was amazing! loved the experience and the team i was able to work with. the perks of working for spotify was helpful and so many free goodies i was able to give out to people! it was amazing.
fun workplace. very pleasant and professional environment. indispensable to work with other departments and adapt to new changes and improvements in the work process.

fun place to work. spotify is a great place to work, especially if you ' re a music fan. the culture is very open and communicative. there are many perks like free lunches, concerts, and social activities that make spotify one of the best places to work.

not what it was. free lunch, free healthcare. paranoid culture. long hours. out of touch management. i joined spotify at a time where we still had that techy - start up feel, although we were 10 years old. it was still a place where although you worked hard, you played harder and there were an abundance of socializing activities that bonded you to your colleagues across the various functions. management was great ( they were mostly swedish ) and you truly felt they cared for and helped you professionally ( career development ) as well as personally if need be. sadly, this is no longer the case.
excellent. excelent place to work... the office has brand new mac books and apple desktop computers, drinks, snacks, fruits, literaty everythng available for you at the office!!!

design ops admin. free lunch, free snacks, multiple work events that were fun and inclusive, ability to travel internationally. overall pretty good work environment and culture, everyone is super innovative and excited about the work they are doing. i think there was some mismanagement with my supervisor but ultimately pretty positive.
dream job. innovative, scrappy - a place where you can do your best work. great way to integrate who you are as a unique person with your career. and, you get to listen to live music!

real productive meet and greet session. free accessories. not enough to learn. starting off with dance lessons in the meet and greet sessions. alongside, was the free premium access to spotify. the positive part about working in spotify was there was no specific desk job and hence was flexible and accessible from anywhere. lots of free accessories were given like drinking cups and ping pong balls. it was really easy since everyone was aware of the campaign. only thing was needed is to sign up people.

very flexible, productive place to work. spotify loosely follows the engineering videos they have published, with respect to the way their structure is laid out. each squad has a very high level of autonomy and there is a great deal of trust in the organization. movement between disciplines is easy, as is movement between teams. so, you can learn a great many things, if you so desire.

you ' ll have fun and be underpaid. spotify is awesome, the culture is unmatched, however, the pay is probably some of the lowest in the market. if you can get over that, you ' ll enjoy the benefits of meeting some of the best in music and working with awesome people.

perfect experience. it is a typical tech / star - up working environment. it ' s fast - paced, self - driven and atonomous. it is a well - paid intern position, but the likelihood of returning is pretty low.

fast pace workplace with tons of benefits. free food. compensation. fast pace environment causing a very stressful one. work and life balance is hard to attain. responsibilities piled up but compensation was not re evaluated. the work culture is fun and employee focus but only if you have the time to enjoy those benefits.
spotify. fun place to work. management has a tough time being transparent. pays very well for the work that is done. the culture is very relaxed and there are always good vibes in the workplace
great. this was a great learning position that allowed me to grow as a business professional. it was enjoyable to work with people as well as develop communication skills

laid back but incredibly productive and friendly. free personalised lunch every wednesday. the ac seems to be on all the time, gets a little cold. the workplace culture was unlike any i have experienced before. the workers were friendly, the atmosphere was calm ( thanks to the music that plays almost constantly ) and there is always food at the office. the employees are of greatly varying ages and it made up of a highly diverse team of diligent workers. while i was there i saw that there are numerous opportunities for advancement within the company - partially because the company is so young, but also due to the management style that makes it so that views and voices are heard my the higher ups.

fun place with nice people, horrible corporate work culture. fun place with nice people, horrible corporate work culture. expect your manager to say things like ' you work when we tell you to work, the hours on your contract are just a guideline ".
great place to work. great place to work. very fun and relaxed company culture and office. lack of advancement opportunities depending on your team. excellent benefits and perks.
great place to work. working for spotify has by far been the best professional experience i have ever had. i have had multiple opportunities to develop myself during the time i have been there.

long hours with no advancement. pros : - great perks like premium spotify, free food, and alcohol - great brand on your resume cons - long working hours from 7 am to midnight for 7 days a week - no work life balance - management doesn ' t care about the wellbeing of employees - no advancement and give false promises - frown upon when takes vacation or lunch break ( end up losing vacation days ) - all the great party are fake because managers won ' t let you go so depends on what team you are in overall, it ' s great for someone who wants to work for one year and get the experience then move on to somewhere better. too much restructuring and mid - management is inexperience. talents are hired from other great companies but this company has poor onboarding and hr so beware!

great work environment. free lunches. becoming official full time employee can seem like a drag. everyone is great and super helpful. the work is autonomous and there is no micromanaging from higher ups breathing down your neck which is a relief. definite resume booster and would definitely recommend to anyone considering applying.

streamlined and modern. spotify capitalized on making my time with them very efficient. the work request was well thought out and the managers were very competent and happy to be working for spotify. you will definitely be happy if you land a role with this company!

dynamic and fast paced. amazing culture and facilities. love the culture and the quickness at spotify. the people are passionate about the product and making the most of their time at work to balance their life outside the company.

amazing. very hard to come across a company better than this one. great management, great benefits, great atmosphere - - work load could be volatile. some days are busier than others. took three months to get an interview and another two to get hired. onboarding can be a process.

management. free lunch / healthcare, 401k matching, coworkers, stock options ( pre ipo ). pay, management. management operates from a fear based pov, and are very difficult to speak honestly with. satellite offices also have significantly less opportunity for career growth than new york, so be cautious that it takes a lot of timing luck and months / years of harder work to get promoted in regional offices.

great place to work, a lot of learning happens here. smart people who can mentor you, great culture, great benefits, career advancement. too much leadership, not enough direction for products, does not do enough to keep highly talented people on board. i went in as a quality assurance engineer and left an ios developer. the amount of learning you can do from within spotify is enormous. if you learning something new, you can, and there are people willing to help you level up your skill set and tool box. the people there are what i enjoyed the most working at spotify. everyone is super smart and they have deep knowledge of what they were hired to do. but, there are too many in leadership positions and some teams suffer from a lack of direction. also, depending on your manager, spotify allows you to " embed " with other teams if you wanted to do something new other than the work on the team you were assigned. interviewing here, is difficult. if you want to work here, you have to be the best, or know someone close to the team you want to work on.
fun. i work as a freelancer and i work from home here. i ahave a lot of fun doing projects for the company with team, as a freelancer i have time for my personal projects too.
great workplace. great culture to learn and grow in. you have autonomy over your career and they emphasize on driving your own development. spotify is the best place i ' ve worked thus far.

amazing! free breakfast, lunch & endless snacks. temporary contract, long interviewing process. people are lovely here & so welcoming! felt very happy and a family - like vibe which is really nice when gong into a new workplace. food & drink provided is amazing, along with endless snacks, free macbook pro, and the work space is amazing.

fun place to work. spotify is probably one of the best places i have worked for so far. i have high regard for the team i had the opportunity to work with. the company has a an amazing work cuture with a good work life balance. if you are on ft, they have amazing benefits.

productive fun place to work with free lunch! my job as a production manager was to insure that all aspects of the video content were met so that we could go out and shoot the video pieces that need to be shot for the campaign at the time. they work culture is very collaborative but it can also be pretty cut throat as everyone there is fairly young ( 25 - 30 ) and is looking to advance themselves as quick as they can. coming in as a new person you can feel out of the loop as most people that have been there a while can be very ( cliquey ). overall decent place to work, the pay is very competitive, benefits are great and you get free music account!

amazing tech company. great office, great product and great opportunities. long hours lead to challenges with work / life balance. workplace culture was excellent. lots of fitness classes, as well as free food and many events.

productive and fun workplace. spotify offers great benefits including paternity and maternity leave, flexible workplace options, entertaining workspaces, and free snacks and daily lunches. spotify encourages diversity and inclusion and provide employees the ability to join internal workplace groups that focus on a variety of important topics that ultimately support the company ' s culture.

a great place to work. i loved the time in spotify, it ' s not perfect and living in new york or stockholm is not the easiest, but it ' s a great place for young or older people, women or undefined people, anybody.

overall a terrific company to work for! great compensation, work / life balance, benefits, room to grow & great culture. the people are bright, friendly and extremely talented across all business units.

one of the worst management. great company & values. average benefits. great nyc location but absolutely horrible middle management. too many people wanting to be superstars and there is no sense of belonging or team.

productive and kind. they were kind to be able to allow you to work where ever as long as you got your work done. you also were given bi - weekly reviews to be able to understand how well you ' re doing.

the luckiest contractor ever? i ' ve worked in a contract capacity for the past 2 years while completing graduate - level coursework. the flexibility offered to me is unique, but it seems from interactions with my team that this spirit of " employee - first " outlook is across the board. i ' ve learned so much and i enjoy being with my team and others in the workplace. the on - site catering and other perks like free tickets to shows ( obv. not during covid ) are really nice, but the best part is being part of a group of people who are just so excited to be there.
productive and fun. great place to work! cant wait to continue to grow more within the company. they are going far! there is no one else like them out there! great employees as well
productive and fun place to work for. productive and fun place to work for, they care and treat their employees with respect! more companies should follow their lead! especially with their...
fun workplace filled with politics. the culture at spotify is something like i ' ve never seen at any other company - - the people are truly bright and passionate, but also know how to have fun

great environment for creatives and business minded individuals. free lunches, smart like - minded individuals, company perks. enjoyed every moment at spotify, colleagues were very helpful, culture was welcoming, and my everyday task were very manageable. the company prioritizes their employees in every aspect.

cohesive & place to thrive. people & perks. work hours longer than expected. spotify is loaded with learning opportunities and growing potential. there are ample perks not offered at most american companies and freedom that is not allowed in other work environments. great place, brilliant co - workers.
great team. it was a great experience to intern at spotify. everyone was really welcoming and inclusive! highly recommend

fun workplace, cool domain. trips to sweden, great perks, good people, great tech infra. comp is a little low, raises are low, re - orgs every six months. great people, a cool domain and pre - pandemic / ipo a lot of great perks. work culture has frayed some and top - down management has compounded the problem. still a fun job
i loved it here. spotify has it faults but it ' s mainly team dependent. my time here was great and i ' ve made some long lasting friendships here. i kind of miss being here.

it ' s an awesome co to work with. gigs. none. a cool working environment ; awesome colleagues ; considerate management ; a balanced work and life experience. this is an lob that you would be looking forward to go back to work the following day aas you always enjoy while working.

great work culture. great place to work at - career and development means a lot to spotify and employees are encouraged to further their trainings and education among other things to boost their skillsets

great work environment but lack of direction. loved working at spotify, highly enjoyed my time. had great co workers but felt constant re - orgs and changes in direction without transparency to be disorienting
great place to work! great place to work, awesome benefits! great teams, collaboration and growth opportunities. i was able to use the skills i learned at spotify at a new employer as the hr manager.

poor leadership, no sense of direction. private healthcare. very poor management, no trust of employees. a company that grew too fast, overhired and then sacked some of the workforce not based on performance. toxic workplace culture with constant micromanagement of employees, very little work due to budget cuts.
great to work and employees friendly. great place to work with, employees are awesome. team is great. stressful at times but work life balance is almost there every time. absolutely amazing

best company culture for growth. growth and connections are encouraged. a lot of freedom and learning opportunities are presented to you, and a bunch of fun music benefits. overall, great place to work.

spotify. great culture, office life, and benefits. interesting industry but not many opportunities for career progression / promotion. would still highly recommend spotify as a great company to work for.

good to r & d employees. good culture of not overworking and burning out, healthy work life balance encouraged. it can be difficult to move up, but i like that moving up isn ' t automatic based on seniority, and that the culture isn ' t hierarchical and overly focused on your job title or level. you are able to and expected to contribute at any level. teams feel self driven and independent, not a lot of top - down oversight.
culture. most people at spotify are amazing. however, spotify is like any corporation and even though they say they care about diversity and inclusion they do not. it is all for show
productive. cool atmosphere and attitude, work - life balance is ok, responsibilities can be given to you if you are good at your job, well paid position, good projects

a company with low pay and little to no experienced managers. inexperienced managers / leaders, pay is low and not competitive. depending on the team you ' re part of there are inexperienced managers and leaders. if you don ' t stroke the egos of the right people, your job will be on the line. the compensation is not competitive and if you want to work at a company that over - compensates with " company culture " to cover the low pay and you can afford to live paycheck - to - paycheck then this is the perfect company for you. the company lacks diversity and it ' s rare that bipoc employees are promoted to leadership positions unfortunately the majority of the company tends to keep bipoc employees in lower - level positions. also, don ' t bother working hard because it ' s not recognized depending on your team. the inexperienced manager that i, unfortunately, had was almost always " loud and wrong " ( i. e. speaking in meetings just to speak but never adding value ).

sweatshop with benefits. food and benefits, status. literally everything else. their turnover rate when i was there in 2021 was at 50 %. you read that right. they lose half their staff every year and for a very good reason you ll work harder and longer than in most any other job you ve had, and under chaotic conditions. they sell it as controlled chaos but don t buy it no one has any idea what s going on, they don t care about you, everyone is disposable, all they care about is the bottom line. you ll get free food and good benefits, the pay is acceptable if a little underwhelming ( especially given the workload ). if you can make it a year you ll have that glossy spotify name on your resume which does open doors, but i ve never felt more abused by a company in my life, and i ve worked in some difficult places.

productive place to work. coworkers were nice. company pays very well and is considerate of their employees work - life balance as well as their health. many perks came with the position but no work place is perfect. on a positive note, you can see that they tried to keep their employees happy to improve profiency and have a positive work environment.
productive and fun. awesome location and would recommend to other. management awesome as well. if you decide to work here prepare for a lot of work but at the end it will be worth it
great interview. had a great experience interviewing with them, everyone was friendly and made sure i wasn t nervous. even though i didn t get the job seems like a good place to work at.

great place to learn and grow, hard to make a big impact. the culture is one of the best out there. the pay and benefits are good but not maang level. work - life balance is great. being remote means you can choose where in the world to work but you ' d miss out the sense of belonging, cool offices and major social events. the kind of work, complexity and sense of impact varies depending where you end up working. the good thing is that with internal mobility is relatively easy to move and try different areas or even try different roles before doing the transition. overall a great place to work.
productive and fun workplace. i loved working at spotify i had a lot of fun with my coworkers. i would definitely recommend working here the job is so fun and everyone is so welcoming.

this company has severe ethics issues. used to be a paycheck, but no raises for a years. everything else. the company had the stock price double this year. the company had a massive profit over last year. the company had a price race and a massive surge of subscribers. however they had a huge layoff. now they are complaining about efficiencies... making people do 3 - 5 different jobs and massive burnout. it is best to avoid this company as they don ' t care about workers, about customers and about anything other than money.

great people and great company. the benefits are amazing, free health insurance. received equity at signing, food credit every month. they fly all new employees to sweden to see the headquarters. just a great job.
great company overall. positive and empowering culture and interesting things to work on. would still be there if not for layoffs. would not go back though as layoffs were such a shock.

great people, fun projects, but not confident in leadership. the people here are great. they aren ' t selfish, and just want to build the best product. but we often get hampered by our leaders who impose their strong beliefs without explaining their rationale. so it can derail a project and cause a lot confusion on the ground.
good company bad leaders. very good company. the leaders don ' t have much clue what they are doing. the chase fancy things without thinking whether users want those or not. the pay is not great
good. good working environment. clear communication and friendly colleagues. this is the best company i have ever worked at! a company that cares about its employees.

low comp, no career growth, poor managers. do not come here if you want to grow as an engineer. do not believe the linkedin hype. they are the best at pr and marketing. it is considered the lowest compensation for engineers in the industry. there is no annual performance bonus, no stock refreshers, and no annual cost of living increase. as of august 2023, they no longer encourage climbing the career ladder. the only path to promotion is by waiting for an internal role to open and to apply for the role like other employees. however, they also removed all of the open roles before they launched this new career enlightenment. it is remote, but most people still go to the ny office. unless you are willing to go into the office, you will not be given impactful work and you will not feel included in your day to day work. you are only able to meet your team once every two years. there is no technical prowess, and you will become stagnant in your career because you will not be able to learn anything new or be innovative. as an engineer, you are expected to build whatever the designers decide because product knowledge is not strong here. product managers rely on designers to dictate product direction. you should only come here if you are tired of thinking for yourself, you are ready to retire, and focus on work life balance.

great company, poor management, no comradely in a lot of departments. what is the best part of working at the company? the benefits and free food. unlimited vacation days, great healthcare, generous parental leave what is the most stressful part about working at the company? the management is terrible. poor leadership, passive aggressiveness all around, discrimination via company culture. what is the work environment and culture like at the company? toxic, dog eat dog. no loyalty amongst co - workers and thought leadership is only accepted if coming from a swedish source / hq in stockholm. the culture vulturing to appear woke is cringey. what is a typical day like for you at the company? there is either zero direction or extreme micromanaging, and not much in between. meetings are held for the sake of just having meetings, and people s job descriptions do not match what they are asked to do.
great company. great place to work, i enjoyed being there. sometimes can be a bit hectic especially in busy periods, but overall would recommend being there. people are great, benefits are great.

total compensation and total insecurity. free food, coffee bars, great vacation time. lack of direction, layoffs. spotify was my dream job. the interview process is very competitive. once you land a role at spotify, the total compensation package was incredible. not just salary, but so much vacation, great health benefits, rsus and cash incentives and grocery stipend - oh my! my team and colleagues were smart and collaborative and fun. i truly loved every day. then i was part of the december 2024 reduction in force. i will likely never find a role as interesting and with such top notch compensation again. be wary - if it seems to good to be true, it might be too good to be true.

changing remote work policy and employees don ' t work very hard. was good but getting much worse. remote work policy is no longer work from anywhere ( wfa ). there are lots of caveats and many requests have been denied. employees who has been there for long time seems very entitled and still thinks it ' s good old days in tech world. employees based in europe are out at least 1 - 2mths, and no kidding. during that time the americans pick up the slack. yes, they do take those leave and productivity is very low across the company.

great place to work with industry standards. fun to be part of an industry leading group. good working environment, remote work felt productive. pay was pretty good when we hit our targets. enjoyed my time.

supportive coworkers and manager. worked here for over 5 years and it has been a great experience overall. colleagues are always really helpful and very competent at what they do. supportive managers, who are eager to help you grow. over the years it was time to go as internally it was difficult to find other opportunities after company size shrunk.
amazing place to work at! the company ' s culture is on the high standard. people are amazing! generally it is an amazing place to work. you should be in the office 3 days out of 5.
fantastic place to intern. my favorite internship to date - - learned so much and loved everyone i worked with. the other interns were a fantastic group of people to work with as well.
fun workplace. very engaging workplace, you feel like there s always something new you can learn and the environment is overall healthy. everyone kinda enjoys the job.

content farm. the work was fun and exciting at first - - and my immediate co - workers were amazing - - but it was nothing more a content farm. we churned out as many scripts as possible to meet the deadline, which meant creativity suffered. also, management wasn ' t very transparent on state of shows and would give little to no notice on if a show was getting cancelled. but again, my co - workers were all fantastic.

good workplace. good employer, has consistently demonstrated exceptional professionalism, dedication, and a strong work ethic. since joining our team, they have quickly become a reliable and valued contributor, consistently delivering high - quality work and going above and beyond expectations.
fun and rewarding. great place to work. greak benefits. very friendly work environment. free lunch even for contract workers. senior managers are a hit and miss but still decent.

good culture. spotify s culture is rooted in innovation, collaboration, and a relentless focus on users. at its core, spotify fosters an environment where creativity thrives, empowering employees to take bold bets, challenge the status quo, and continuously learn. the company embraces a think it, build it, ship it, tweak it mindset, valuing speed and experimentation over perfection. cross - functional collaboration is key, with squads and tribes working autonomously while staying aligned to a shared mission of unlocking the potential of human creativity. transparency and trust are integral, enabling open communication and psychological safety where ideas can flow freely. diversity and inclusion are celebrated, ensuring different perspectives shape product and business decisions. spotify also prioritizes well - being, offering flexibility and supporting a healthy work - life balance. above all, the culture is driven by passion for music, podcasts, and technology, uniting employees in creating meaningful experiences for creators and listeners worldwide.

fun and creative work opportunities. i love working for them when making contracted music. it ' s so fun and really opens up the eye of music. not really any restrictions as far as what you can and can ' t make, just be you.

i want to earn on spotify. a typical day at work involves balancing tasks, meeting deadlines, and collaborating with colleagues. i believe i ve learned valuable skills in time management, teamwork, and problem - solving. management provides direction, though communication can always be improved. the workplace culture fosters collaboration and adaptability, which motivates everyone to do their best. the hardest part of the job is handling pressure during peak workloads, while the most enjoyable part is achieving goals as a team and seeing results from hard work.

the spotify is a great app fto you to listen songs. nothing. there is not downsides. a typical day at work involved collaborating with my team, organizing daily tasks, and solving unexpected challenges. i learned how to manage my time efficiently, communicate clearly, and stay calm under pressure. management was supportive and open to feedback, always encouraging professional growth and teamwork. the workplace culture was positive, inclusive, and focused on mutual respect and cooperation. the hardest part of the job was balancing multiple priorities during busy periods, but it helped me improve my problem - solving skills. the most enjoyable part was seeing the results of our efforts and working with great colleagues who motivated each other every day.
